answer:
**Manu** (*Manusmṛti*, Book 1) catalogues the four *varṇas* in hierarchical order of origin from the body of the Self-Existent One: (1) Brāhmaṇa from the mouth, (2) Kṣatriya from the arms, (3) Vaiśya from the thighs, and (4) Śūdra from the feet.[^1] Their core duties (*dharma*) are correspondingly fixed: Brāhmaṇas to teach and study (among other Vedic rites); Kṣatriyas to protect the people; Vaiśyas to protect cattle and engage in trade/agriculture; and Śūdras solely "to serve these other *varṇas* without malice."[^2]

**Viṣṇu** (*Viṣṇu Smṛti*, early chapters, especially ch. 2) likewise orders them: (1) Brāhmaṇa, (2) Kṣatriya, (3) Vaiśya, and (4) Śūdra.[^3] Duties are parallel but summarized more concisely: Brāhmaṇas to teach; Kṣatriyas to practice arms; Vaiśyas to tend cattle; Śūdras to serve the twice-born.[^4]

Viṣṇu's treatment admits **more mobility between *varṇas***, explicitly permitting "in times of distress, one may adopt the livelihood of the next lower *varṇa*."[^5] Manu allows some occupational flexibility in distress (detailed later, in ch. 10), but Book 1 stresses innate, irrevocable hierarchy without such proviso.

**Rigidity shows up most explicitly in Manu**, via the body-part origin (elevating the mouth-pure Brāhmaṇa as "lord of this whole creation" and entitled to all property), the Shudra's singular servile duty "without malice," and Brāhmaṇas as "the best" of all beings by birth alone.[^6] Viṣṇu implies hierarchy (e.g., Śūdras serving "twice-born") but lacks such vivid, absolutist declarations in its early chapters.[^7]

[^1]: *Manusmṛti* 1.31, [para. p-13a2db](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-13a2db).
[^2]: *Manusmṛti* 1.88–91, [paras. p-71a5c2 to p-26a572](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-71a5c2:p-26a572).
[^3]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 2.1, [para. p-883be2](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-883be2).
[^4]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 2.5–8, [paras. p-354ee5 to p-f34593](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-354ee5:p-f34593).
[^5]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 2.15, [para. p-600695](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-600695).
[^6]: *Manusmṛti* 1.31, 1.91–93, 1.100, [paras. p-13a2db, p-26a572, p-7c0113, p-cc748e](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-13a2db:p-cc748e).
[^7]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* chs. 1–2, [ch. 1](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/), [ch. 2](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/).
